text: What part of 'no' do you not understand? I've said it repeatedly!
style_prompt: Speak with a Angry emotion at a moderate speed and high pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'no', stress the word 'repeatedly', and insert a pause after 'understand'.

text: You can't just walk in without knocking! Respect other people's space.
style_prompt: Speak with a Angry emotion at a moderate speed and moderate pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'can't', stress the word 'Respect', and insert a pause after 'knocking'.

text: The creature’s eyes glowed from the underbrush, unyielding and ancient.
style_prompt: Speak with a Fearful emotion at a fast speed and moderate pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'glowed', stress the word 'unyielding', and insert a pause after 'underbrush'.

text: Grease congealed on the cold plate, like a slug stopped mid-trail.
style_prompt: Speak with a Disgusted emotion at a slow speed and low pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'congealed', stress the word 'slug', and insert a pause after 'plate'.

text: I’ve told you a thousand times! How many more before you listen?
style_prompt: Speak with a Fearful emotion at a fast speed and moderate pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'thousand', stress the word 'listen', and insert a pause after 'times'.

text: You expect me to eat this after seeing how it's prepared?
style_prompt: Speak with a Disgusted emotion at a moderate speed and low pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'eat', stress the word 'prepared', and insert a pause after 'this'.

text: And suddenly, without a warning, the statue's eyes blinked slowly.
style_prompt: Speak with a Surprised emotion at a fast speed and high pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'suddenly', stress the word 'blinked', and insert a pause after 'warning'.

text: Traffic droned like a rolling grumble, blanketing the city in annoyance.
style_prompt: Speak with a Angry emotion at a moderate speed and high pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'droned', stress the word 'annoyance', and insert a pause after 'grumble'.

text: That cloud just spelled my name—how could anyone explain something like that?
style_prompt: Speak with a Surprised emotion at a moderate speed and moderate pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'spelled', stress the word 'explain', and insert a pause after 'name'.

text: Every fork was encrusted, as if dipped in a sorrowful chocolate shell.
style_prompt: Speak with a Disgusted emotion at a moderate speed and low pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'encrusted', stress the word 'sorrowful', and insert a pause after 'fork'.

text: The trash bag ripped, spilling a rancid liquid that clings to your shoes.
style_prompt: Speak with a Disgusted emotion at a moderate speed and moderate pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'ripped', stress the word 'rancid', and stress the word 'clings'.

text: Each word felt like a burden, each sentence choked back, heavy with unshed tears.
style_prompt: Speak with a Sad emotion at a slow speed and moderate pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'burden', stress the word 'heavy', and insert a pause after 'back'.

text: You bought a car without telling me first? That's... A bit unexpected.
style_prompt: Speak with a Surprised emotion at a moderate speed and high pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'car', stress the word 'unexpected', and insert a pause after 'first'.

text: Paint peeled off the wall, stress lines like a permanent frown etched into the surface.
style_prompt: Speak with a Angry emotion at a fast speed and high pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'peeled', stress the word 'permanent', and stress the word 'frown'.

text: Raindrops meandered down the pane, like tears on forgotten cheeks.
style_prompt: Speak with a Sad emotion at a slow speed and low pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'meandered', stress the word 'tears', and insert a pause after 'pane'.

text: We built these dreams, brick by brick, only for them to crumble like sand.
style_prompt: Speak with a Sad emotion at a moderate speed and moderate pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'dreams', stress the word 'crumble', and insert a pause after 'brick'.

text: Did you just say you're leaving tomorrow? That's... Sooner than i thought.
style_prompt: Speak with a Sad emotion at a slow speed and low pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'leaving', stress the word 'sooner', and insert a pause after 'tomorrow'.

text: She left the door ajar, a cold draft whispering secrets of days gone by.
style_prompt: Speak with a Sad emotion at a moderate speed and low pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'ajar', stress the word 'whispering', and insert a pause after 'ajar'.

text: The sun’s harsh glare glinted off the metal, uncompromising and relentless.
style_prompt: Speak with a Angry emotion at a fast speed and high pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'harsh', stress the word 'uncompromising', and stress the word 'relentless'.

text: The echoes faded, leaving nothing but the whisper of my sigh.
style_prompt: Speak with a Sad emotion at a slow speed and low pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'faded', stress the word 'whisper', and insert a pause after 'faded'.

text: The car door slammed shut, punctuating the end of the argument.
style_prompt: Speak with a Angry emotion at a moderate speed and moderate pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'slammed', stress the word 'punctuating', and insert a pause after 'shut'.

text: A brisk breeze carries the scent of pine and earth, invigorating and fresh.
style_prompt: Speak with a Happy emotion at a moderate speed and high pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'brisk', stress the word 'invigorating', and stress the word 'fresh'.

text: Every rustle from the trees seemed to whisper her name.
style_prompt: Speak with a Fearful emotion at a moderate speed and high pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'rustle', stress the word 'whisper', and stress the word 'name'.

text: The silence in the house was heavy after the argument.
style_prompt: Speak with a Disgusted emotion at a slow speed and low pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'silence', stress the word 'heavy', and insert a pause after 'house'.

text: The streets are quiet now, kissed by rain, every droplet a tear shed silently.
style_prompt: Speak with a Sad emotion at a moderate speed and moderate pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'quiet', stress the word 'tear', and insert a pause after 'now'.

text: But we apply ourselves to the history of its production.
style_prompt: Speak at a moderate speed and moderate pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'apply', stress the word 'history', and stress the word 'production'.

text: He would have withdrawn from the feast had not the noise of voices allayed the smart.
style_prompt: Speak at a fast speed and moderate pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'withdrawn', stress the word 'allayed', and insert a pause after 'feast'.

text: 'Well, do you know if the persons you see there are rich or poor, if their sacks of wheat are not rubies or diamonds?
style_prompt: Speak at a moderate speed and moderate pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'know', stress the word 'rich', and stress the word 'poor'.

text: 'Yes,' said he, 'if it belonged to me only, you should have it.'
style_prompt: Speak at a moderate speed and high pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'belonged', stress the word 'only', and insert a pause after 'he'.

text: 'We got to take our chances of gettin' by, that's all.
style_prompt: Speak at a moderate speed and moderate pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'got', stress the word 'chances', and insert a pause after 'by'.

text: Aye, aye! you may hang for all I care, whether you are a ghost or whatever you are.' So he passed on with his ox.
style_prompt: Speak at a fast speed. Specifically, stress the word 'hang', stress the word 'care', and stress the word 'ghost'.

text: I am only making it thus bald that you may know and face the worst.'
style_prompt: Speak at a slow speed. Specifically, stress the word 'bald', stress the word 'know', and stress the word 'worst'.

text: Fray Sibyla was about to seat himself without paying any more attention to these protests when his eyes happened to encounter those of the lieutenant.
style_prompt: Speak at a slow speed. Specifically, stress the word 'seat', stress the word 'attention', and insert a pause after 'himself'.

text: The voice from the hall below was loud and gruff.
style_prompt: Speak at a fast speed. Specifically, stress the word 'loud', stress the word 'gruff', and insert a pause after 'below'.

text: That's life enough for me at present-no more, no less.
style_prompt: Speak at a slow speed. Specifically, stress the word 'life', stress the word 'present', and insert a pause after 'me'.

text: 'I don't just understand you, Anne,' he said hesitatingly 'You appear to be vexed about something.'
style_prompt: Speak at a low pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'just', stress the word 'vexed', and insert a pause after 'Anne'.

text: 'Father,' he kept crying, 'dear father, how he insulted you!' And I sobbed too.
style_prompt: Speak at a high pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'dear', stress the word 'insulted', and insert a pause after 'Father'.

text: It was a long knife and many scalps were tied on the handle.
style_prompt: Speak at a low pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'long', stress the word 'scalps', and insert a pause after 'knife'.

text: When esther was dressed, she and mrs Saunders knelt down and said a prayer together.
style_prompt: Speak at a low pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'knelt', stress the word 'prayer', and insert a pause after 'dressed'.

text: They began to descend; Monsieur Defarge going first with the lamp, mr Lorry closing the little procession.
style_prompt: Speak at a low pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'descend', stress the word 'first', and insert a pause after 'descend'.
